messages.content: Across the last 7 days, list each decision discussed. Under each, bullet the supporting arguments and opposing arguments with speaker tags when possible.
Searching your lifelogs from the last 7 days for discussions about decisions...
I'm going to extract decisions mentioned across the past 7 days and list supporting/opposing arguments with speaker tags. Searching entries...

args.query: decision OR החלטה OR decision about contract OR agreement OR hire OR schedule change OR 'מה ש' OR 'החלטה' OR 'decide'

messages.content:
Nice—you're asking for a clean list of every decision discussed in the last 7 days, with supporting and opposing arguments and speaker tags. Got it. I combed your lifelogs from 2025‑09‑19 → 2025‑09‑26 and pulled the concrete decisions that were discussed. Below: each decision as a header, then bullets of supporting / opposing arguments with available speaker tags (when a speaker name wasn't explicit I label as Unknown or You where appropriate). Short, precise — straight to the point.

1) Approve contract appendix wording (definitions for roles: clinical manager / portfolio manager / psychiatrists) and updated physician payment terms
- Supporting
  - Unknown (likely colleague): “I can approve most of it — but we need clarity on the definition of מנהל תיק (portfolio manager).” — wants the document sent to review.  
  - You (Rami): prefers broad definitions for clinical roles; will draft a clear email with specific wording and changes (e.g., doctor fee numbers: \"after stage B change 100/200 → 112.5/225\"). Rami is proposing a concrete edit and to send tomorrow.
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: notes some definitions and annotations (from Dekel) may be incorrect for insurance and role qualifications (e.g., portfolio manager should be MSc psychologist or similar) — requests explicit verification before sign‑off.

2) Send final contract / appendices today (urgent approval flow)
- Supporting
  - Unknown: “I need your approval, it’s urgent — I want to send it before the doctors finish.” — pushes for immediate sign‑off.
  - You: agreed to review the most up‑to‑date files and respond.
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: flagged unresolved annotation/comments in the doc; requests more careful check (esp. clinical role definitions) before final dispatch.

3) Who owns operational management of the new tele‑psychiatry “on‑call” flow (Rami to own vs. hand off / add operational manager)
- Decision discussed: Rami should be the clinical owner but hand operational day‑to‑day to an ops/project lead; need escalation/gaps while Rami is unavailable.
- Supporting
  - You (Rami): accepts responsibility for the project clinically and agrees to run it, but recognizes operational limits while traveling.
  - Unknown colleague: insists project must have a clear operational owner (ravi/roei/shahar/Alinor suggested as ops supports) so nothing falls between chairs. Recommends Rami remain accountable but appoint an operational lead.
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: noted assumptions that others would perform tasks created a gap; when Rami was on travel, training was missed. Argument that unless ownership and escalation paths are explicit, tasks fall through.

4) Fix immediate incident: patient summary & consent flow after telemedicine case (open ticket / manual workaround)
- Decision discussed: create manual fix today (open record, send summary) and implement process changes + drills.
- Supporting
  - Unknown (Rami / Operations): “We can manually open the record, insert the summary, request patient email and send the summary.” — practical immediate remedy.
  - Unknown (team): propose two actions: 1) immediate manual closure and summary send; 2) set up escalation loop/automation & run drills (incl. nights) to validate.
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: concerns about consent/legal: if the automatic pipeline wasn’t used (no signed consent), must ensure patient consent before sharing; legal/operational nuance.
  - Unknown: note that solving technic is easy, but the real failure was unclear ownership and missing check that training completed.

5) Add a full Thursday shift / change weekly schedule (specific team)
- Supporting
  - Unknown: “I increased Thursday to a full day.” — intention to add coverage; speaker says they will also work that day.
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: logistical confusion — “how to get patients there / arrangements?” and “we didn’t plan the handoffs” — some pushback because arrangements not fully thought through.

6) Run clinician webinar / training for partner therapists about the on‑call service
- Supporting
  - You: scheduled a webinar to explain clinical use cases, when to call the service, and how it integrates into existing workflows; proposed times (Thursday or specific slot).
  - Unknown colleagues: welcome; suggested including Ra'ut (partner clinic) in drills and ensuring they feel confident.
- Opposing / concerns
  - None substantial — everyone agreed training is necessary; timing and attendees to be confirmed.

7) Implement escalation / acknowledgement loop in on‑call alerting (if psychiatrist doesn't pick up)
- Supporting
  - Unknown (operations): need an automated loop: if psychiatrist doesn’t confirm receipt, escalate to next person / send alternate alert. This will prevent missed calls at 02:00.
  - Unknown (Rami): supports drills and automation; wants visibility for himself / Dekel / Roi on who is on call.
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: technical complexity and need to confirm phone number capture and link behaviors (some partner forms failing to capture patient email/phone made previous incident worse). Also needs legal clarity on consent capture mechanism.

8) Who manages the AI grader / agenda project & prompt engineering work (task ownership + training)
- Supporting
  - You and colleagues: decide the work should be driven by project owner but use GPT/Gemini/playground for rapid drafting; recommend the UX owner learn prompt engineering course (14 hours) and build prompts themselves to understand outputs.
  - Unknown: Suggests building product specification / requirement doc using LLMs, then run model evaluations.
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: warns that an inaccurate grader can break the whole validation pipeline; requires careful human validation and evaluation design — so don’t rush to automation.

9) Vehicle repair / parts installation logistics (multiple small decisions)
- Supporting
  - Unknown: decisions to install specific car parts (intercooler, snorkel, cabin modifications) and where to have parts fitted (local garage vs. order from abroad).
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: availability of parts, cost, and risk that custom parts require international ordering (e.g., eBay / Spain / Japan) and that installation may be expensive or impractical.

10) Property / land investment options (explore and not rush to sell)
- Supporting
  - You: explore options (buy, rent, develop with partner, solar station, food trucks / ad revenue mixes) — suggests long term hold vs quick flip, run numbers (expected NOI).
  - Unknown: ran scenarios estimating rental yield (e.g., 3–4% to 4.5% depending on location) and proposed mixed revenue streams to boost returns.
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: permits, upfront capex, difficulty of segmentation, and risk the land may have building restrictions (setbacks from street) — caution against rushing purchase/sale.

11) Team structure for UX / AI / Automation — who does concept design vs PM vs UX
- Supporting
  - You: want UX/expert to translate data to recommendations, then hand tasks to product/technical owners; notes project vs product distinction (we operate by projects and outcomes).
  - Unknown: suggests keeping roles: concept design, UX, clinical, automation, tech — and a project manager to route tasks.
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: question whether a single person (project manager) can be both concept designer and PM — recommends split responsibilities.

12) Short‑term: manual workaround + tests & future: scheduled drills (incl night drills) for on‑call flow
- Supporting
  - Unknown ops & You: everyone agreed on immediate manual fix and formal testing schedule: internal drills + partner (Ra'ut) drills at normal and off‑hours.
- Opposing / concerns
  - Unknown: workload and availability for drills (need to coordinate).

— That’s the list of decisions discussed in the past 7 days from your lifelogs. Want this exported as a one‑page checklist you can send to the team with owners, deadlines and next actions? I can draft the exact email/messages for Rami → Operations → Shahar/Roi → Dekel fast. Pick the recipients and I’ll draft.
